title: The Yule Ball
prose: As part of hosting the Tournament, Hogwarts is holding a Yule Ball on Christmas Day — fourth-years and up allowed to attend, younger students allowed only if someone older asks them, which turns the whole run-up to Christmas into a minor social crisis for half the school.

The Great Hall is transformed for the night: real ice sculptures, a thousand fairy lights strung between silver-frosted trees, the Weird Sisters playing loud enough to feel through the floor. Dress robes that looked ridiculous in the shop somehow look right under the enchanted ceiling's soft, star-strewn light.

Whatever the rest of the year turns out to hold, this one night is unambiguously, simply good — dancing badly, laughing about it, and for a few hours not thinking about Dark Marks or dragons or anything at all beyond the next song.

choices.label: You worked up the nerve to ask someone yourself.
Someone else worked up the nerve first.
You went with a group of friends, no dates involved.
